channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🏴 From Moscow to Crimea, Ukraine is winning the drone war against Russia | Atlantic Council
item.description: As Russia’s full-scale invasion enters a fifth summer, it is becoming increasingly apparent that the tide in the drone war has turned in Ukraine’s favor, writes David Kirichenko. The post From Moscow to Crimea, Ukraine is winning the drone war against Russia appeared first on…

item.title: 🏴 Russia’s New Two-Seat Su-57 Felon Takes Its First Flight | The War Zone
item.description: Official imagery confirms that the two-seat Su-57D version of the Russian fighter has begun flight testing. The post Russia’s New Two-Seat Su-57 Felon Takes Its First Flight appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: ⚛️ Space Force names Sandhoo as head of new missile warning/tracking PAE | Breaking Defense
item.description: As of May 11, Sandhoo is double hatted as both head of the PAE and director of the Space Development Agency.

item.title: 🛡️ NORTHCOM standing up ‘Nordic Bridge’ to boost US coordination in Arctic | Breaking Defense
item.description: Gen. Guillot said, “The goal is always to defend as far away from the homeland as we can” and that special operations forces will play a key role.

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine’s experience can teach Europe how to defend against Russia | Atlantic Council
item.description: Ukraine's wartime transformation should inform European defense modernization in both technology and doctrine. Europe must now choose: Seize the opportunity to adapt or procrastinate and risk future defeat, write Valeriya Ionan and Nicolas Dunais. The post Ukraine’s experience…

item.title: 🔐 Hackers using AI just found a ‘zero-day.’ The spyware industry is watching. | Atlantic Council
item.description: Spyware companies that hack into devices for the purpose of surveillance and data extraction are primed to exploit this new opportunity. The post Hackers using AI just found a ‘zero-day.’ The spyware industry is watching. appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏛️ Air Force grounds T-38 fleet after Mississippi mishap | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Air Force said an “operational pause” is necessary as an investigation into a May 12 crash of the aircraft proceeds. Inspections to return aircraft to flight are expected to begin as soon as this week.

item.title: 🏛️ Navy, Marine Corps back longer amphib readiness cycles, request more ships | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Navy currently utilizes a 36-month Optimized Fleet Response Plan (OFRP), but leaders want to stretch it to 56 months.

item.title: 🏴 What Does Putin Want From Xi? | Foreign Policy
item.description: Russia seeks to resolve several trade issues during this week’s summit. But China holds the cards.

item.title: 🏴 A Ukrainian ground robot defended a position from Russian assault for six weeks | Defense One
item.description: UGVs are beginning to replace infantry on Ukraine’s front lines.

item.title: 🏛️ War in pieces: Air Force wants special ops plane that can be built on the fly | Defense News
item.description: The Skyraider II, a militarized version of the AT-802 crop duster, is built to give isolated special ops teams eyes overhead and firepower on call.

item.title: 🌏 Washington Might Be Ready to Bargain With Beijing | Foreign Policy
item.description: The United States may be accepting the reality of Chinese power.

item.title: 🏴 Sweden Chooses Unusual French Design For Its New Frigates | The War Zone
item.description: The security situation in Europe led Sweden to choose the in-production FDI design, but it will be packed with Swedish weapon systems. The post Sweden Chooses Unusual French Design For Its New Frigates appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 After the Iran war, the Gulf’s next economic phase awaits | Atlantic Council
item.description: The region is no longer defined by a single economic model based on energy extraction, but by a set of diverging strategies shaped by common constraints. The post After the Iran war, the Gulf’s next economic phase awaits appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌍 Why the Iran war is breaking the US‑European strategic alliance | Defense One
item.description: The continent is learning that it must not count on Washington.

item.title: 🏛️ Navy’s MQ-25 Stingray gets green light for low-rate initial production | Breaking Defense
item.description: A low-rate initial production contract for three aircraft is expected this summer, the Navy said.

item.title: 📍 MQ-25A Stingray cleared for deployment, says Cao | Defense News
item.description: The role of the MQ-25A is intended to provide the Carrier Air Wing with unmanned refueling, freeing up F/A-18E/F aircraft to focus on strike missions.

item.title: 📍 BlitzBox Packs 100 Weaponized Drones Into An Unassuming Container | The War Zone
item.description: DZYNE's BlitzBox is the kind of containerized drone launcher the U.S. military is now very actively pursuing. The post BlitzBox Packs 100 Weaponized Drones Into An Unassuming Container appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌏 The impacts of the Iranian crisis on Japan’s energy strategy | Atlantic Council
item.description: Strategic planning has enabled Japan to manage the Hormuz energy crisis relatively well. But the shock also exposed policy weaknesses that need to be addressed to improve the country's energy resilience. The post The impacts of the Iranian crisis on Japan’s energy strategy…

item.title: 🌏 Asian Reactions to Trump’s Summit with Xi | War on the Rocks
item.description: From May 14 to 15, U.S. President Donald Trump held a summit in Beijing with Chinese leader Xi Jinping. In addition to pageantry, the summit featured discussions about Iran and the Strait of Hormuz, Taiwan, and bilateral trade. Both Washington and Beijing emphasized a…

item.title: 📍 Shield AI tapped to integrate autonomous software on LUCAS drone | Breaking Defense
item.description: The company says it will integrate its Hivemind software on the drones, with plans to demo its swarming capability later this year.

item.title: 📍 The US needs a comprehensive batteries strategy to ensure its battlefield edge | Atlantic Council
item.description: Batteries are becoming increasingly important in defense technologies. To keep an operational edge, the US needs to focus on new supply chains and developing technological advantages. The post The US needs a comprehensive batteries strategy to ensure its battlefield edge…

item.title: 🏴 More US troop withdrawals from Europe expected, NATO commander says | Defense News
item.description: Gen. Alexus Grynkewich said Europe should "absolutely" expect additional U.S. troop withdrawals as NATO allies provide more of their own defense.

item.title: 📍 Can Armenia’s Democracy Prevail? | Foreign Policy
item.description: Unconditional Western support for the incumbent prime minister could backfire.

item.title: 📍 B-1B’s Future Armament According To Its Test Pilot | The War Zone
item.description: The Bone is getting set to spend the last decade of its career with a whole set of new arrows in its quiver. The post B-1B’s Future Armament According To Its Test Pilot appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ Diving into Golden Dome’s new pricetag, plus winning the Army’s network wars | Breaking Defense
item.description: This week on The Break Out, we review the latest tensions over Golden Dome’s forecasted costs before visiting Colorado where special forces and infantry linked up for a novel exercise at Fort Carson.

item.title: 🏛️ Plan To Test OA-1K Skyraider II’s Rapid Deployability Outlined By Air Force Special Ops Command | The War Zone
item.description: The Air Force also plans to add laser-guided rockets to the OA-1K’s armament as it works toward operational capability amid relevance concerns. The post Plan To Test OA-1K Skyraider II’s Rapid Deployability Outlined By Air Force Special Ops Command appeared first on The War…

item.title: 📍 Three Concrete Steps to Advance Palestinian Freedom | Foreign Policy
item.description: Legal reform and structural change may lack flair, but they can improve the situation on the ground.

item.title: 🌏 The Geopolitical Circumstances of Japan-South Korea Rapprochement | The Diplomat
item.description: The latest Lee-Takaichi summit highlighted not only their warm personal rapport but their priorities: energy and economic security.

item.title: 📍 Litigation Tracker: Legal Challenges to Trump Administration Actions | Just Security
item.description: A public resource tracking all the legal challenges to the Trump administration's executive orders and actions. The post Litigation Tracker: Legal Challenges to Trump Administration Actions appeared first on Just Security.
